meta.title: Nook Listing Engine
meta.subtitle: Seller-listing lead plan — shared board for Jen + the AI worker
meta.worker: Claude · hourly · exits quietly when nothing is actionable

legend.jen: For Jen — do it, then mark done (button here or tell Claude). Completing it can unlock AI tasks.
legend.ai: For the AI worker — picked up automatically on an hourly run once unblocked.

tasks.title: Create the GitHub repo
tasks.detail: Go to github.com/new → Repository name: nook-listing-engine → Public → Create repository (no README needed). Then tell Claude your GitHub username.

tasks.title: Create a repo token and paste it to Claude
tasks.detail: github.com → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Fine-grained tokens → Generate new token. Name: nook-worker. Repository access: Only select repositories → nook-listing-engine. Permissions → Repository permissions: Contents = Read and write, Issues = Read and write. Generate, copy, and paste it into any Claude chat in this project.
tasks.result: Done — connected via Zapier GitHub instead (paste-token route retired; revoke the token if you haven’t)

tasks.title: Publish the board + website to GitHub
tasks.detail: Push all files, set repo links on the board, verify everything renders.
tasks.result: Board + site published to the repo ✓

tasks.title: Turn on GitHub Pages
tasks.detail: Repo → Settings → Pages → Source: Deploy from a branch → Branch: main, folder / (root) → Save. Site appears at <username>.github.io/nook-listing-engine in ~2 minutes.

tasks.title: Open the live board and bookmark it
tasks.detail: Confirm the site loads and the Mark-done buttons work (they open a pre-filled GitHub issue — just click Submit).

tasks.title: Draft the “How We Launch Your Listing” page
tasks.detail: The listing-marketing page none of the top-3 competitors has: photography/drone/twilight, video tour, staging consult, single-property site, Zillow Showcase, targeted Meta ads, luxury syndication. Written for nook-realty.com + reusable in listing presentations.

tasks.title: Review the listing-marketing page draft → publish
tasks.detail: Approve/edit the draft, then publish to the site (or send to your web person). This page also becomes the spine of your listing presentation.

tasks.title: Send 4–6 past luxury solds for the proof gallery
tasks.detail: Addresses + sold prices you're comfortable showing (career range runs to $5.4M — that proof is currently invisible online). Photos if you have them.

tasks.title: Draft the luxury sold-gallery page + credential blurbs
tasks.detail: Sold gallery copy plus the credential stack (CLHMS, ~$65M career, $5.4M top sale, years in DS) in versions for: website header, IG bio, email signature, listing deck.

tasks.title: Confirm CLHMS membership is current
tasks.detail: Log in at luxuryhomemarketing.com and check status — luxury sellers check the directory, and you're currently not findable in it.

tasks.title: Draft review-request batch #1 (engineered prompts)
tasks.detail: Personalized drafts to past closed clients, prompting property type / neighborhood / days-on-market mentions so reviews do SEO work. Drafts only — nothing sends without approval.

tasks.title: Approve & send review batch #1
tasks.detail: Goal: 25–40 Zillow reviews by Oct 31 (you have 8; the local benchmark is 77).

tasks.title: Confirm Google Business Profile owner access
tasks.detail: Check business.google.com — do you have (or can you claim) the Nook Realty / Jen Ferrara profile?

tasks.title: Draft the GBP optimization package
tasks.detail: Categories, description targeting “Dripping Springs listing agent”, services, posts cadence, review link wiring.

tasks.title: Zillow: claim Zillow Pro + pick the canonical profile
tasks.detail: Zillow Pro is free for Preferred partners through 2026. You have 3 fragmented profiles; pick the keeper (likely the 5.0★ one) so consolidation can start.

tasks.title: Draft Zillow profile consolidation + optimized copy
tasks.detail: The consolidation request to Zillow support + rewritten profile copy (Nook branding, luxury + DS positioning, review strategy).

tasks.title: Verify RealScout subscription (active? who pays?)
tasks.detail: Determines the valuation-funnel engine: RealScout Home Value Alerts if active, budget fallback if not ($179–299/mo if we'd have to start paying).

tasks.title: Spec the instant home-valuation funnel
tasks.detail: Landing copy + flow with the timeframe qualifier (now / 3 months / 12 months / just curious — stolen from NestHaven) routed to matching FUB stages and Flodesk nurture speeds. No competitor offers an instant valuation — this is the open lane.

tasks.title: Draft the DSF seller pages
tasks.detail: “What's My DSISD Home Worth?” page + gated 2026 DSISD Seller's Guide built on the price-cut narrative (63.5% of DS listings cut price in Q2).

tasks.title: Review & publish the DSF seller pages
tasks.detail: Approve the drafts, publish on drippingspringsfamilies.com, and swap the Azulio form per the exit plan.

tasks.title: Meta check: campaigns running? Business Manager access?
tasks.detail: Two answers needed: are any Meta campaigns currently live, and do you have Ads Manager / Business Manager login access? (Zero Meta-source leads are reaching Nook queues.)

tasks.title: Draft the Meta seller-campaign package
tasks.detail: 3 ad variants (valuation + market-report angles incl. the price-cut hook), homeowner audience spec for DSISD/Wimberley/Driftwood zips, budget split from existing spend, FUB source tagging.

tasks.title: Research 3 micro-farm neighborhood candidates
tasks.detail: 300–400-home luxury pockets (DS acreage, Lake Travis, Lost Creek) with turnover rates and medians, so the pick is data-based.

tasks.title: Pick THE micro-farm neighborhood
tasks.detail: One neighborhood, quarterly mailer, 18–24 month horizon. ~$85–135/mo.

tasks.title: Draft postcard #1 (copy + Canva design brief)
tasks.detail: Just-solds + hyperlocal stat + QR to the valuation page so the farm is measurable.

tasks.title: Register wimberleyfamilies.com (~$12/yr)
tasks.detail: It looked unregistered on 7/22. Grab it before someone else does — any registrar works.

tasks.title: Scaffold the Wimberley site
tasks.detail: Page map cloned from the DSF playbook + first 3 community profiles drafted.

tasks.title: Video engine: 8-week calendar + script #1 + editor brief
tasks.detail: “What actually sold in DSISD this month” format. Phone-shot, pro-edited (~$100–200/video). Every video ends on the valuation CTA. For luxury sellers, the videos ARE the listing presentation.

tasks.title: Film video #1 (script provided)
tasks.detail: ~90 minutes: shoot on phone per the script, send the raw file back for editing.

tasks.title: Research DS ELITE Real Estate Professionals membership
tasks.detail: Criteria, dues, who runs it, how to apply. It's visibly how NestHaven's top producers source referral business.

tasks.title: Join DS ELITE (if dues make sense)
tasks.detail: Zero-ad-spend listing channel; also scouts the Wimberley/Driftwood equivalent.

tasks.title: Build the builder/developer target list (5 names)
tasks.detail: Small DS/Driftwood/Wimberley builders, acreage developers, land sellers — the Sentinel Peak effect (Veritas turned ONE developer relationship into 26 luxury homesites). Contact + angle for each.

tasks.title: Book 2 builder/developer coffees
tasks.detail: From the target list — the highest-leverage hours in the whole plan.

tasks.title: Build the luxury referral-circle list (10 contacts)
tasks.detail: Wealth managers, estate & divorce attorneys, builders, architects, stagers in Westlake/Lakeway/DS — with a short outreach note drafted for each. Life events drive luxury listings.

tasks.title: Book the first 2 referral-circle meetings
tasks.detail: One coffee/lunch per month is the pace; these are the first two.

tasks.title: Set up the $750K+ expired/withdrawn routine
tasks.detail: Tracker template, daily check workflow for Jen's territories, hand-written-note draft, compliance checklist (confirm off-market, no exclusive, DNC). The $2M+ stall makes this list unusually rich right now.

tasks.title: Draft the Zillow rep email (Showcase quote + Pro)
tasks.detail: Request Showcase pricing (~$30–80/listing) and confirm Zillow Pro enrollment. Draft only — queued for approval.

tasks.title: Approve & send the Zillow rep email
tasks.detail: One click in Gmail once the draft is ready.

tasks.title: Find the Zillow Preferred agreement
tasks.detail: Two numbers we need: the success-fee % and whether the relationship is portable if you change brokerage. Blocks the broker-license decision.

tasks.title: Draft the Sprout admin ask (FUB controls + $750K+ routing)
tasks.detail: What can Jen control in the shared FUB (Lead Flow, action plans, integrations), plus a request for a $750K+ → Jen routing rule on Nook-bound leads. Draft only.

tasks.title: Approve & send the Sprout ask
tasks.detail: Determines whether speed-to-lead automation gets set by you or requested from Sprout.

tasks.title: Run the stage-hygiene sweep (report only)
tasks.detail: Nook-scoped FUB read: who's sitting in “Lead” stage too long, proposed stage moves, speed-to-lead gaps. Report goes to the private project (no client names on this public board); writes wait for approval. Zillow Preferred scores this — stage hygiene is revenue.

tasks.title: Approve stage fixes + Meta→FUB feed fix
tasks.detail: Say yes to the proposed stage moves and the Facebook Lead Ads → FUB reconnection.

tasks.title: Write the data-backed “Best Realtors in Dripping Springs 2026” article
tasks.detail: NestHaven's version has zero data. Ours cites verifiable review counts, sales volume, and DOM — honest, useful, and it outranks theirs. Includes Jen in honest context.

tasks.title: Review & publish the Best-Realtors article on DSF
tasks.detail: Sensitive one — read it fully before it goes live.

tasks.title: Write acreage-seller article #1
tasks.detail: “Selling acreage in the Hill Country: wells, septic, ag exemptions, wildlife valuations.” Attacks the ranch/land authority all three competitors lean on. Ends on the valuation CTA.

tasks.title: Ask Sprout/ERA what luxury syndication is included
tasks.detail: Before paying for JamesEdition-style syndication, check what the brokerage/ERA network already provides.
